chapter_context.summary: Contains the famous poetic meditation on the season for all things, contrasts human mortality and divine eternity, and notes the equal physical fate of men and beasts.

outline.text: The Poem of Times: A Season and Time for Every Purpose
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Poem of Times: A Season and Time for Every Purpose (3:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ecclesiastes.

outline.text: God's Perfect and Eternal Work Contrasted with Human Limitations
outline.explanation: This section focuses on God's Perfect and Eternal Work Contrasted with Human Limitations (3:9-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ecclesiastes.

outline.text: Injustice in the World and Common Physical Fate of Men and Beasts
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Injustice in the World and Common Physical Fate of Men and Beasts (3:16-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ecclesiastes.

key_themes.title: Life Under the Sun
key_themes.description: The phrase 'under the sun' refers to the purely horizontal, physical world, highlighting the vanity of seeking ultimate meaning in mortal pursuits alone.

key_themes.title: The Gift of the Present
key_themes.description: Despite the vanities, the Preacher consistently advises rejoicing in the present moment, loving families, enjoying food, and taking pride in one's labor as direct gifts from God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the search of Chapter 3, in what ways have you sought ultimate fulfillment in 'vanity' (pleasures, wealth, achievements) rather than in God?
How does this chapter encourage you to slow down, enjoy your daily portion, and trust in the sovereign timing of the Creator?

cultural_historical.title: Qoheleth (The Preacher)
cultural_historical.text: The author calls himself Qoheleth, traditionally translated as 'The Preacher' or 'The Assembly Leader', exploring deep philosophical and theological struggles in late Old Testament history.
